steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The students claim cataract frequency differences are solely attributable to Aasdh expression variation. To weaken this, a finding must show that Aasdh variation alone does not determine cataract frequency — i.e., different expression levels produce the same outcome.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — wild-type mice with wide variety of Aasdh expression show cataract frequencies similar to knockout mice: proves that varying Aasdh expression doesn't change the cataract outcome, directly undermining the 'solely attributable' claim. ✓
  • Choice B — supports the conclusion: confirms the students' claim rather than weakening it. ✗
  • Choice C — confirms variation exists without attacking causation: shows variation is real but doesn't challenge whether it's the sole cause. ✗
  • Choice D — strengthens the experimental design: makes the experiment more credible, which supports the conclusion. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
